2604.08970.title: Litmus (Re)Agent: A Benchmark and Agentic System for Predictive Evaluation of Multilingual Models
2604.08970.paper_summary: We study predictive multilingual evaluation: estimating how well a model will perform on a task in a target language when direct benchmark results are missing. This problem is common in multilingual deployment, where evaluation coverage is sparse and published evidence is uneven across languages, tasks, and model families. We introduce a controlled benchmark of 1,500 questions spanning six tasks and five evidence scenarios. The benchmark separates accessible evidence from ground truth, enabling evaluation of systems that must infer missing results from incomplete literature evidence. We also present Litmus (Re)Agent, a DAG-orchestrated agentic system that decomposes queries into hypotheses, retrieves evidence, and synthesises predictions through feature-aware aggregation. Across six systems, Litmus (Re)Agent achieves the best overall performance, with the largest gains in transfer-heavy scenarios where direct evidence is weak or absent. These results show that structured agentic reasoning is a promising approach to multilingual performance estimation under incomplete evidence.
2604.08970.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究預測性多語言評估：在缺乏直接基準結果的情況下，估計模型在目標語言上執行任務的表現。這個問題在多語言部署中很常見，因為評估覆蓋範圍稀疏，且發表的證據在語言、任務和模型家族之間不均衡。我們引入了一個包含1,500個問題的受控基準，涵蓋六個任務和五種證據情境。該基準將可獲得的證據與真實情況分開，從而使得評估必須從不完整文獻證據中推斷缺失結果的系統成為可能。我們還介紹了Litmus (Re)Agent，一個由DAG協調的代理系統，將查詢分解為假設，檢索證據，並通過特徵感知聚合合成預測。在六個系統中，Litmus (Re)Agent實現了最佳的整體表現，特別是在直接證據薄弱或缺失的轉移重度情境中獲得了最大的增益。這些結果表明，結構化的代理推理是在不完整證據下進行多語言表現估計的一種有前景的方法。
2604.08970.author: Avni Mittal et.al.
2604.08970.authors: Avni Mittal, Shanu Kumar, Sandipan Dandapat, Monojit Choudhury
